title: Australia Adult Passport Renewal
description: Renewal of an adult Australian passport from within Australia through the Australian Passport Office's online application portal, printed and lodged at a participating Australia Post outlet. This document describes the eligibility conditions for renewal (as opposed to a new/first-time application), the applicant and contact-detail inputs, the current-passport and photo inputs, and the processing-speed and payment choices. It does not submit the application; the live passports.gov.au sources are always authoritative (see VERIFICATION.md). Applying from outside Australia is a separate process, out of scope here.

authority.name: Australian Passport Office, Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema authored from the official passports.gov.au adult-passport, passport-photos, identity-documents, and fees pages, retrieved via Wayback Machine snapshots after passports.gov.au and dfat.gov.au blocked direct automated retrieval at the TLS layer (see VERIFICATION.md). Eligibility, photo, and fee/payment-channel facts were read directly from those official pages. The granular applicant/contact/previous-passport field set was modelled on the corroborated pattern shared with the published gb/hmpo, ie/dfa, and ca/ircc passport-renewal schemas, cross-checked against secondary renewal-process walkthroughs, because the AusPassport online form itself sits behind an authenticated session the review could not reach. Status remains 'draft'. Fees are intentionally not encoded as authoritative data.

fields.name: issuedAgeSixteenOrOlder
fields.label: Was your current passport issued when you were aged 16 or older?
fields.description: One of the conditions for renewing rather than applying for a new passport.
fields.sourceRef: Eligibility for passport renewal — issued when you were aged 16 or older

fields.name: issuedOnOrAfterJan2006
fields.label: Was your current passport issued on or after 1 January 2006?
fields.description: One of the conditions for renewing rather than applying for a new passport.
fields.sourceRef: Eligibility for passport renewal — issued on or after 1 January 2006

fields.name: issuedWithAtLeastTwoYearsValidity
fields.label: Was your current passport issued with at least 2 years' validity?
fields.description: One of the conditions for renewing rather than applying for a new passport.
fields.sourceRef: Eligibility for passport renewal — issued with at least 2 years' validity

fields.name: personalDetailsUnchanged
fields.label: Are your name, date of birth, place of birth, and gender the same as on your current passport?
fields.description: Renewal requires your name, date of birth, place of birth, and gender to be unchanged since your current passport was issued. A change of any of these (e.g. by marriage, deed poll, or gender transition) means you must apply for a new passport instead, a separate process out of scope here.
fields.sourceRef: Eligibility for passport renewal — current name, date of birth, place of birth and gender

fields.name: passportNotLostStolenOrCancelled
fields.label: Has your current passport been reported lost, stolen, or cancelled?
fields.description: Must be false (not reported lost, stolen, or cancelled) to be eligible for renewal; you must also present this passport when you lodge your application. If it was lost, stolen, or cancelled, use the replacement passport process instead, out of scope here.
fields.sourceRef: Eligibility for passport renewal — not be reported lost, stolen, or cancelled; be presented when you lodge your application

fields.name: currentPassportStatus
fields.label: Is your current passport still valid, or has it expired?
fields.description: current = still valid; expired = expired. Renewal is available in either case; APO's published eligibility criteria state no maximum time since expiry, unlike some other jurisdictions' online-renewal windows.
fields.sourceRef: Eligibility for passport renewal — have expired or still be valid

fields.name: surname
fields.label: Surname
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Must match your current passport (see personalDetailsUnchanged).
fields.sourceRef: Application form — applicant details: surname

fields.name: givenNames
fields.label: Given names
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Must match your current passport (see personalDetailsUnchanged).
fields.sourceRef: Application form — applicant details: given names

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Date of birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Full date, YYYY-MM-DD.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — applicant details: date of birth

fields.name: placeOfBirthCity
fields.label: Place of birth — city or town
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — applicant details: place of birth

fields.name: placeOfBirthCountry
fields.label: Place of birth — country
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Australia for most applicants; see identity-document requirements for applicants born outside Australia.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — applicant details: place of birth

fields.name: sex
fields.label: Sex
fields.description: Recorded in the 'Sex' field of an Australian passport. M = male, F = female, X = indeterminate/intersex/unspecified. Must match your current passport (see personalDetailsUnchanged); applying with a different marker than your current passport means you must apply for a new passport instead, out of scope here.
fields.sourceRef: passports.gov.au — Change of name or gender: sex and gender diverse passport applicants

fields.name: residentialAddressLine1
fields.label: Residential address — line 1
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. This document covers applying from within Australia; a different process applies overseas, out of scope here.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — contact details: residential address

fields.name: residentialAddressLine2
fields.label: Residential address — line 2
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — contact details: residential address

fields.name: residentialSuburb
fields.label: Residential address — suburb
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — contact details: residential address

fields.name: residentialState
fields.label: Residential address — state or territory
fields.description: Australian state or territory of residence.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — contact details: residential address

fields.name: residentialPostcode
fields.label: Residential address — postcode
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — contact details: residential address

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Used for the AusPassport online account and for delivery notifications (email plus an SMS Registered Post tracking number).
fields.sourceRef: Application form — contact details: email

fields.name: phone
fields.label: Contact phone number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. In international E.164 format, e.g. +61412345678.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — contact details: phone number

fields.name: currentPassportNumber
fields.label: Current Australian passport number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. The passport you are renewing; you must present it when you lodge your application, and it will generally be cancelled at lodgement.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — current passport details: passport number

fields.name: currentPassportIssueDate
fields.label: Issue date of your current passport
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Full date, YYYY-MM-DD. Used to confirm the issuedOnOrAfterJan2006 and issuedWithAtLeastTwoYearsValidity eligibility conditions.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — current passport details: date of issue

fields.name: currentPassportExpiryDate
fields.label: Expiry date of your current passport
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Full date, YYYY-MM-DD. Used to confirm the currentPassportStatus and issuedWithAtLeastTwoYearsValidity eligibility conditions.
fields.sourceRef: Application form — current passport details: date of expiry

fields.name: photo
fields.label: Passport photos
fields.description: 2 identical printed colour passport photos meeting APO's photo guidelines: 35-40mm wide, 45-50mm high, face (chin to crown) 32-36mm, less than 6 months old, plain white or light background, neutral expression, no glasses (absent a medical exemption). Write your full name in black pen on the back of one photo. This field models the printed photos that accompany the lodged form, not a digital upload.
fields.sourceRef: Passport photos — photo size, quality, and full-name endorsement

fields.name: passportValidityRequested
fields.label: Passport validity requested
fields.description: ten_year = standard 10-year validity passport, available to all eligible applicants; five_year = optional 5-year validity passport at a reduced fee, available only to applicants aged 75 or over. Confirm current fees on the live source.
fields.sourceRef: Passport fees — 10-year validity passport (persons aged 16 and over); 5-year validity passport (optional for persons 75 and over)

fields.name: processingSpeed
fields.label: Processing speed
fields.description: standard = routine processing, at least 6 weeks from lodgement; priority = Priority service, processed within 2 business days of receipt, for an additional fee; fast_track = Fast Track service, processed within 5 business days of receipt, for an additional fee. Priority and Fast Track require a complete application and exclude postage/delivery time. Confirm current fees on the live source.
fields.sourceRef: Renewing a passport — Step 4: faster processing (Priority / Fast Track); Passport fees

fields.name: paymentMethod
fields.label: Payment method
fields.description: Paid when you lodge your application at Australia Post. eftpos = EFTPOS; mastercard = MasterCard; visa = Visa; amex = American Express; cash = cash. Confirm the current fee on the live source.
fields.sourceRef: Passport fees — How do I pay the passport fee? At Australia Post - EFTPOS, MasterCard, Visa, AMEX or cash

steps.title: Passport validity, processing speed, and payment
